q.en: What kind of story is "Coyote and the Star"?
q.zh: 《郊狼与星星》是一个什么样的故事？

options.en: A science textbook
options.zh: 一本科普教科书

options.en: A news report about space according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 一篇关于太空的新闻报道（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: A biography of a wolf
options.zh: 一本关于狼的传记

options.en: A Klamath Native American folktale that explains how Crater Lake formed
options.zh: 一个克拉马特印第安人的民间故事，解释火山口湖的由来

explain.en: The book is "A Klamath Native American Folktale" and says it is "a myth told to explain the origin of Crater Lake."
explain.zh: 书中标明它是“克拉马特印第安人民间故事”，并说这是“用来解释火山口湖由来的神话”。

q.en: Why is Coyote known as the "Song Dog"?
q.zh: 为什么郊狼被称为“歌犬（Song Dog）”？

options.en: Because he was a good hunter
options.zh: 因为他是个好猎手

options.en: Because he was silent
options.zh: 因为他很安静

options.en: Because he loved to howl and sing, even though his voice was scratchy
options.zh: 因为他喜欢嚎叫和唱歌，尽管嗓音有些沙哑

options.en: Because he lived with birds according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 因为他和鸟儿住在一起（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The book says, "Coyote was known among the other animals as the Song Dog, even though his voice was rather scratchy. He loved to howl and sing his songs."
explain.zh: 书中说：“在其他动物中间，郊狼以‘歌犬’闻名，尽管他的嗓音相当沙哑。他喜欢嚎叫、唱自己的歌。”

q.en: How did Coyote first try to get the star to notice him?
q.zh: 郊狼最先是用什么办法让星星注意到他的？

options.en: By catching a fish
options.zh: 抓一条鱼

options.en: By composing and singing a beautiful new song over three days
options.zh: 用三天时间创作并唱了一首优美的新歌

options.en: By building a fire
options.zh: 生一堆火

options.en: By writing a letter according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 写一封信（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: Coyote "thought and wrote. After three days he had composed a new song" and sang it to the star, hoping she would notice him.
explain.zh: 郊狼“思考并写词。三天后他创作了一首新歌”，对着星星唱，希望她能注意到自己。

options.en: Why the moon is bright
options.zh: 月亮为什么明亮

options.en: How wolves became dogs according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 狼是如何变成狗的（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: How to climb a mountain
options.zh: 如何爬山

options.en: The origin of Crater Lake and why coyotes howl at the stars
options.zh: 火山口湖的由来，以及郊狼为什么对着星星嚎叫

explain.en: The book ends by saying the story explains the origin of Crater Lake, and that "when coyotes howl at the stars, they are scolding the star that killed the first Coyote."
explain.zh: 书的结尾说这个故事解释了火山口湖的由来，并说“当郊狼对着星星嚎叫，是在责骂那只害死第一只郊狼的星星”。

q.en: How long did Coyote fall after the star let him go?
q.zh: 星星松开手后，郊狼坠落了多久？

options.en: For seven nights according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 七个夜晚（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: For one whole moon, twenty-eight days
options.zh: 整整一个月亮周期，二十八天

options.en: For one hour
options.zh: 一个小时

options.en: For three days
options.zh: 三天

explain.en: The book says, "For the time of one whole moon, twenty-eight days, Coyote fell to the Earth."
explain.zh: 书中说：“在整整一个月亮周期、二十八天里，郊狼一直坠向地面。”

q.en: What words describe Coyote when he kept going after tripping and tumbling?
q.zh: 当郊狼绊倒、滚下山坡仍继续前进时，书中用什么词形容他？

options.en: Resilient and determined
options.zh: 坚韧而坚定

options.en: Noisy and rude
options.zh: 吵闹又无礼

options.en: Lazy and sleepy
options.zh: 懒惰又爱睡

options.en: Frightened and still according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 害怕且不动（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: After tumbling down a hill, "But Coyote was resilient. And he was determined to meet the beautiful star."
explain.zh: 滚下山坡后，“但郊狼很坚韧。他下定决心要见到那颗美丽的星星。”

q.en: What happened when Coyote hit the ground?
q.zh: 郊狼撞到地面时发生了什么？

options.en: He woke up from a dream
options.zh: 他从梦中醒来

options.en: He turned into a star
options.zh: 他变成了一颗星星

options.en: His impact made a hole that filled with his blood-turned-water, becoming a deep blue lake
options.zh: 撞击形成一个大坑，他的血变成水注满坑里，成了一座深蓝色的湖

options.en: He found the missing puzzle piece according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 他找到了缺失的拼图碎片（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The book says his blood "turned to water and filled the hole to become a beautiful lake. It is the deepest, bluest lake on the continent of North America"—Crater Lake.
explain.zh: 书中说他的血“变成水，注满那个坑，成了一座美丽的湖。那是北美大陆上最深、最蓝的湖”——即火山口湖。

q.en: Why does the author say coyotes now "scold the star" when they howl?
q.zh: 作者为什么说郊狼现在嚎叫时是在“责骂那颗星星”？

options.en: To show they remember the first Coyote's love and the star that let him fall
options.zh: 为了说明它们记得第一只郊狼的爱，以及那颗让他坠落的星星

options.en: To teach readers to fear stars according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 为了教读者害怕星星（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: To describe the night sky
options.zh: 为了描写夜空

options.en: To explain how coyotes hunt
options.zh: 为了解释郊狼如何捕猎

explain.en: The book says coyotes howl to scold the star "that killed the first Coyote. They remember him and his great love in their songs."
explain.zh: 书中说郊狼嚎叫是在责骂那只“害死第一只郊狼的星星。它们在歌声里纪念他和他伟大的爱”。

q.en: What did Coyote do to cross the deep, swift river?
q.zh: 郊狼是怎样渡过那条又深又急的河的？

options.en: Climbed out on a tree branch, but it broke and he fell into the water
options.zh: 爬上一根伸过河面的树枝，但树枝断了，他掉进水里

options.en: Built a bridge
options.zh: 搭了一座桥

options.en: Flew over it
options.zh: 飞了过去

options.en: Swam straight across according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 直接游了过去（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: Coyote "climbed the tree and tiptoed out onto the branch. Just as he reached the end... the branch broke. Splash!" He fell into the cold, swift water.
explain.zh: 郊狼“爬上树，踮脚走上树枝。刚走到尽头……树枝就断了。扑通！”他掉进又冷又急的水里。

q.en: How did Coyote feel as he danced high above the Earth with the star?
q.zh: 当郊狼和星星在高高的天上起舞时，他感觉如何？

options.en: Calm and happy
options.zh: 平静又开心

options.en: Dizzy and afraid; he missed his rock and the ground
options.zh: 头晕又害怕；他想念屋外的石头和地面

options.en: Proud and sleepy according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 骄傲又困倦（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Hungry and angry
options.zh: 饥饿又生气

explain.en: High above Earth, "Coyote began to get dizzy and afraid... He begged the star to return him to Earth. He missed the rock outside his house, and he missed the ground."
explain.zh: 在高高的天上，“郊狼开始头晕、害怕……他求星星把他送回地面。他想念屋外的石头，想念大地。”

prompt.en: Coyote wanted something he could not safely have and learned a hard lesson. Tell about a time you wanted something badly and what you learned from not getting it—or from finally getting it.
prompt.zh: 郊狼渴望自己无法安全得到的东西，并由此学到了教训。讲讲你曾经非常想要某样东西，以及你从“没有得到”或“最终得到”中学到了什么。

en: Wanting something does not always mean it is good for us.
Being brave can also mean knowing when to stop.
Love and friendship are best when they are returned.
Stories from long ago often teach lessons about life.
zh: 想要某样东西，并不等于它对我们有好处。
勇敢有时也意味着懂得何时该停下。
爱与友谊，最好能得到回应。
古老的故事常用来传达人生的道理。
